extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) under the condition (hyperkalemia AND seizure_history AND ckd_dialysis); otherwise it defaults to false. Thus rec_drug_a = true requires exactly that conjunction. For rec_drug_b, three things can affect it: guard_suppress_b (priority 90, condition = same conjunction, sets false), rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, condition = hyperkalemia, sets true), and the default (false). The highest-priority matching rule per output wins. Whenever the conjunction holds (the only situation making rec_drug_a true), guard_suppress_b (priority 90) matches and outranks rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), forcing rec_drug_b := false despite hyperkalemia being true. Therefore in every input where rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is false, so (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is never jointly true. The mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire input space.
